serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Gobi Kulcha is one piece, weighing approximately 150 grams.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritive Value of Indian Foods
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values adapted from ICMR-NIN for 'Kulcha' and 'Cauliflower Paratha' to estimate Gobi Kulcha, considering the stuffing.

gi_source.title: International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI estimated based on 'Whole wheat flour roti' (GI 52) and 'Potato paratha' (GI 64), considering Gobi Kulcha's composition of wheat flour and spiced cauliflower-potato filling. The average of these values is 58.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (300mg/100g) and saturated fat from ghee/butter.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should reduce added salt in the dough and filling to bring sodium below 200mg/100g and minimize ghee/butter brushing to lower saturated fat intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (58) and high glycemic load per serving (24.4), primarily due to refined flour and potato in the filling.
type2_diabetes.guidance: To manage blood sugar, reduce potato and increase cauliflower in the filling, use whole wheat flour, and limit portion size to one piece (~80-100g) paired with a high-protein side.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (3.0g/100g) and cholesterol (10mg/100g) from ghee/butter.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: To reduce cardiovascular risk, minimize the use of ghee or butter for brushing and consider healthier oil alternatives; monitor overall saturated fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Gobi Kulcha is a popular Indian unleavened flatbread, similar to naan, distinguished by its savory stuffing of spiced cauliflower (Gobi). The dough, typically made from refined or whole wheat flour, is meticulously prepared, then a flavorful mixture of grated cauliflower, mashed potatoes, and various Indian spices like turmeric, ginger, garlic, and green chilies is enclosed within it. The kulcha is then flattened and traditionally cooked in a tandoor (clay oven) or on a tawa (griddle) until golden brown and slightly puffed. It has a soft, chewy texture with a subtly spicy and earthy filling. Nutritionally, it is a source of carbohydrates and protein, but its glycemic index can be medium to high due to the flour and potato content. It is often brushed with butter or ghee, adding to its fat content. Gobi Kulcha is a vegetarian dish commonly served with yogurt, pickles, or a rich curry.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are an adaptation from ICMR-NIN for similar Indian breads and cauliflower-based dishes, as direct data for 'Gobi Kulcha' was not available. GI is an analogous estimate.
notes: Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and specific fatty acid data for Gobi Kulcha are not readily available in approved sources. Potassium_mg is an estimate based on ingredients.
